zh.lifeSummary: David de St Michael於1175年出生在愛爾蘭萊音斯特迪 本 米亞斯。他於1202年與Margery le Fleming在愛爾蘭庫迪 米亞斯斯藍結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: David de St Michael于1175年出生在爱尔兰萊音斯特迪 本 米亞斯。他于1202年与Margery le Fleming在爱尔兰庫迪 米亞斯斯藍结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: St
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Americanized spelling of French   St  .   Denis  .

variants.name: Denis
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Camille, Jacques, Armand, Marcel, Andre, Pierre, Serge, Yvan, Alphonse, Edouard, Francois, Gaetane.  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Jorge, Roberto, Angel, Francisco, Joaquin, Juan, Manuel, Miguel, Ramon, Rolando.

French, Walloon, Breton, West Indian (mainly Haiti), German, Dutch, English, and Portuguese; Spanish and Galician (Denís): from the personal name Denis, Spanish and Galician Denís, from Latin Dionysius ‘(follower) of Dionysos’ (see Dennis ). Compare Dennee .
Ukrainian and Rusyn: variant transliteration of   Denys  , a cognate of 1 above.

nameMeanings.name: Michael
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, German, Dutch, Jewish, and Greek: from the personal name Michael, ultimately from Hebrew Mika’el ‘Who is like God?’. This was borne by an archangel in the Bible, the protector of Israel (Daniel 10:13, 12:1; Rev. 12:7). In Christian tradition, Michael was regarded as the warrior archangel, conqueror of Satan, and the personal name was correspondingly popular throughout Europe, especially in knightly and military families. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages, e.g. Hungarian Mihály (see Mihaly ), Czech and Slovak Michal , Assyrian/Chaldean or Arabic Mikhael and Mikhail , and also patronymics and other derivatives from these names, e.g. Greek Papamichael , its cognate Papamichalis, and Michaelides , Serbian Mihajlović (see Mihajlovic ), and Slovenian Mihelčič (see Mihelcic ). Compare Mikeal .

variants.name: Miceli
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Angelo, Sal, Vito, Mario, Giuseppe, Luciano, Rosario, Santo, Arturo, Carlo, Giuseppi, Luca, Paolo, Pasquale, Salvador, Aida, Alfredo, Augusto, Ildo, Lino, Marta.
Italian (southern): from a southern variant of the personal name Michele (see Michael ).

variants.name: Micek
meanings: Czech (Míček and Miček), Slovak (Miček), and Polish: from a pet form of personal names Michal, Michał, equivalents of Michael , or Mikuláš, Mikołaj, equivalents of Nicholas . Compare Mica .

variants.name: Mich
meanings: Polish: from a short form of any of various personal names such as Michał (see Michael ) or Mikołaj (see Nicholas ).
German (Rhineland-Palatinate): unexplained.
English: variant of   Mitch   3 or 4. This form of the surname is rare in Britain.

variants.name: Micale
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Carlo, Domenic, Salvatore, Antonio, Ferdinando, Filippo, Natale, Nicola, Orlando, Remo, Rocco, Rosario, Santo.
Italian (southern): from a southern variant of the personal name Michele (see Michael ), influenced by the Greek vernacular form Michalis.

variants.name: McElmeel
meanings: Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Giolla Michil ‘son of the servant (i.e. devotee) of Saint Michael’. The surname contains an earlier borrowing of the Biblical personal name Michael , before the lengthened vowels of the modern Irish Gaelic form Mícheál.

variants.name: McMichael
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Mhichíl, a patronymic from a Gaelic form of the personal name Michael . This was the usual shortened Anglicized form of Mac Gille Michil in Scotland.

variants.name: Mical
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Janina, Jerzy, Mieczyslaw, Zygmund.
Polish (Micał): from a vernacular form of the personal name Michał (see Michael ) or Mikołaj (see Nicholas ).

variants.name: Michaeli
meanings: German and French: Latinized (humanistic) patronymic from the personal name   Michael  .

variants.name: Mica
meanings: Czech (mainly Míča, also Miča) and Slovak (Miča): from Mica and Miča, pet forms of the personal name Michal, an equivalent of Michael , or Mikuláš, an equivalent of Nicholas .
Romanian: from a female personal name derived from the adjective mic, feminine mică ‘small, short’.

variants.name: Mihaly
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Laszlo, Balint, Tamas, Zoltan.
Hungarian (Mihály): from the personal name Mihály, Hungarian form of Michael .
In some cases also an Americanized form of Croatian Mihalj (see Mihal ), a cognate of 1 above.

nameMeanings.name: David
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, borne by the greatest of all the kings of Israel, whose history is recounted with great vividness in the first and second books of Samuel and elsewhere. As a boy he killed the giant Philistine Goliath with his slingshot. As king of Judah, and later of all Israel, he expanded the power of the Israelites and established the security of their kingdom. He was also noted as a poet, many of the Psalms being attributed to him. The Hebrew derivation of the name is uncertain; it is said by some to represent a nursery word meaning ‘darling’. It is a very popular Jewish name, but is almost equally common among Gentiles in the English-speaking world. It is particularly common in Wales and Scotland, having been borne by the patron saint of Wales (  see  Dewi   ) and by two medieval kings of Scotland.

en.lifeSummary: David de St Michael was born in 1175, in Diocese of Meath, Leinster, Ireland. He married Margery le Fleming in 1202, in Slane, County Meath, Ireland. They were the parents of at least 1 son.

fr.lifeSummary: David de St Michael est né en 1175, à Diocese of Meath, Leinster, Irlande. Il a épousé Margery le Fleming en 1202, à Slane, County Meath, Irlande. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils.

de.lifeSummary: David de St Michael wurde 1175 in Diocese of Meath, Leinster, Irland geboren. Er hat 1202 in Slane, County Meath, Irland Margery le Fleming geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: David de St Michael è nato il nel 1175 nel Diocese of Meath, Leinster, Irlanda. Lui sposato Margery le Fleming nel 1202, nel Slane, County Meath, Irlanda. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio.

ja.lifeSummary: David de St Michaelは1175年にアイルランドレインスターディオセゼ オフ メアスで生まれた。1202年にアイルランドコウントイ メアススランでMargery le Flemingと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: David de St Michael(은)는 1175년 아일랜드 레인스터 디오스스 프 미스에서 태어났다. 그는 1202년 아일랜드 카운티 미스 슬랜에서 Margery le Fleming(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: David de St Michael nasceu em 1175, em Diocese of Meath, Leinster, Irlanda. Ele casou-se com Margery le Fleming em 1202, em Slane, County Meath, Irlanda. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho.

ru.lifeSummary: David de St Michael родился в 1175 г. в местности диосес оф мит, линстир, Ирландия. Он женился на женщине по имени Margery le Fleming в 1202 г. в местности слан, коунти мит, Ирландия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын.

es.lifeSummary: David de St Michael nació en 1175, en Diocese of Meath, Leinster, Irlanda. Se casó con Margery le Fleming en 1202, en Slane, County Meath, Irlanda. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo.
